menu.installUnavailable: Installation not available on this device/browser.

mode.title: Mode
mode.notes:
EP: automatic sign (±) and no magnitude clipping.
NR: bounds the ratio to [0,1] and applies ±0.999…990; if there are overflows, you’ll be prompted to redefine Min/Max.

reconv.title: Scale remapping
reconv.tip: Tip: create “Latitude (−90, 90) degrees” and “Longitude (−180, 180) degrees” to reuse them.
reconv.templateApplied: Template applied. Fill in “Value to normalize (observed)”.

errors.revMinMaxSave: Check Min and Max before saving.

results.title: Results
results.note: In the UC Scale, 0 is an unattainable asymptotic limit; we always work with minimal/maximum expressions ±0.000…010 and ±0.999…990.

inverse.title: Enter UC → compute observed
inverse.description: Locks “Value to normalize (observed)” and uses the UC (or extended UC) to reconstruct the observed value using the Min/Max from Remapping.
inverse.blocked: Inverse mode active: enter UC in Results.
inverse.blockedObs: Inverse mode active: the Observed value is locked.
inverse.revMinMax: Fill in Min and Max in Remapping.
inverse.revCU: Enter a valid UC in Results.
inverse.noteNR: In NR, the out-of-range sign is not uniquely reversible; it is assumed to be within range.
inverse.done: Observed computed from UC → sent to Remapping.

formula.title: Applied formula
formula.note: The fraction and the sign update with your inputs and the selected mode.

about.title: What is the UC Scale?
about.intro: The Universal Coherence (UC) Scale is a way to normalize any magnitude (temperature, distance, probability, etc.) into a dimensionless comparable value. Instead of mixing units, you define a reference range (Min and Max) and the app expresses where your Observed value falls within (or outside) that range.
about.coreLi1: You define a range: Min and Max (the reference).
about.coreLi2: You compute the relative position: (Obs − Min) / (Max − Min).
about.coreLi3: You scale the result to an operational maximum (avoiding ±1): × (±0.999…990).
about.rangeP1: In UC, 0 represents an ideal structural center (Cₛ) treated as an unattainable asymptotic limit. That’s why, in practice, we work with an operational minimum: VmO = 0.000000000000010 (and its negative symmetric).
about.rangeP2: The same happens at the upper end: VMO = 0.999999999999990 (and its negative symmetric) is used to keep an asymptotic margin and avoid saturation at ±1.
about.formulaP1: The conversion the app applies is: (Obs − Min) / (Max − Min) × (±0.999…990). The fraction measures the relative position within the range; the factor sets the operational ceiling.
about.formulaP2: The sign is determined as follows: if Obs is within [Min, Max], the result is +. If it is outside, it is marked − (in EP, the magnitude can also exceed 1).
about.modesLi1: EP (Pure Structural): does not clip the magnitude. If Obs moves away from the range, the ratio can be > 1 and the UC can also grow in magnitude (keeping the out-of-range sign).
about.modesLi2: NR (Reference-Normalized): bounds the magnitude to [0,1]. If Obs is outside the range, a warning will be shown and the result will be limited to ±0.999…990.
about.extP1: In addition to the rounded UC, an extended UC with higher precision (50 decimals) is shown for auditing, logging, or exact copying. It’s the same quantity, just with more decimals.

help.title: Help
help.intro: This app converts from Observed → UC (direct mode) or from UC → Observed (inverse mode). The key is defining the reference range (Min and Max) correctly, because that range is the “context” of the normalization.
help.quick2: In Scale remapping, select a Template or type your own range (Min/Max) and, if you want, the observed value.
help.quick3: Press Use in Inputs (or Normalize now) to copy the values to Inputs and see the Applied formula.
help.quick4: Check Results: UC value and Extended UC value are computed. You can copy them with one tap.
help.quick5: If you want to start over, use Clear.
help.resultsLi1: UC value: a compact (rounded) format for quick reading.
help.resultsLi2: Extended UC value: the same value with 50 decimals for maximum precision.
help.resultsLi3: The “pill” with ±0.999…990 indicates the operational factor and the sign used. In general: + = in range, − = out of range (depending on the mode).
help.normLi1: Templates fill in Min/Max (and unit) quickly for common scales (pH, Kelvin, Likert, etc.).
help.normLi2: With ➕ Save as normalization or Save normalization you can store your range and reuse it.
help.normLi3: With Use in Inputs you apply the saved normalization to the calculator without retyping data.
help.normLi4: To clean your list, use Delete in “Saved normalizations”.
help.inverseLi2: Make sure you have Min and Max defined in Remapping (they’re required to reconstruct the observed value).
help.inverseLi3: Choose whether you’ll enter UC or extended UC, type the value in Results, and the app will compute the observed value.
help.inverseLi4: In EP, if the UC is negative (out of range) you can indicate whether it corresponds to < Min or > Max to disambiguate.
help.inverseLi5: In NR, the out-of-range sign is not uniquely reversible; when reconstructing, the app assumes a value within the range.
help.privacyP1: The app runs 100% locally: normalizations and preferences (language/theme) are stored in your device’s browser. No data is sent to any server.

utils.title: Utilities

page1.title: Date → ordinal converter
page1.desc: Interprets UTC and the proleptic Gregorian calendar (year 0 = 1 BCE). Origin ≡ “second 1”.

utils.resultTitle: Click to send to ‘Value to normalize’
utils.tip: Tip: click the result to transfer it to “Value to normalize (observed)”.

errors.targetBefore: Destination must be after or equal to Origin.

lang.title: Language
lang.description: Choose the interface language. Saved content and numeric values are not modified.

themes.title: Themes
themes.description: Choose a visual theme. It applies to the whole app and will be remembered on this device.

errors.equalMinMax: Min and Max cannot be equal.

coherence.title: Universal Coherence
coherence.text: May this work serve as gratitude and as commitment: to think, feel, and act in the same direction, without “ornaments”; to keep creating with ethics, with joy, and with respect for everything that connects us.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body:
OptimeFlow(s) Normaliza Coherente runs 100% locally (in your browser). We don’t send the values you enter to external servers, and we don’t use analytics or trackers.

Data stored on this device

  • Preferences: visual theme and language.
  • Saved normalizations: name, unit, and Min/Max ranges (and, if you save it, the observed value).
  • Offline cache (if you install it as a PWA): the browser may store app files so it can open without a connection.

Browser permissions

  • Clipboard: only used when you press “Copy”.
  • Storage: used to save your preferences and normalizations in localStorage.

Connectivity

To load or update the app, your browser may download the files (HTML/CSS/JS) from the same origin. This does not include sending your usage data. If you enable offline/PWA mode, the Service Worker may cache those files.

How to delete your data

You can remove everything from your browser settings (site data: storage/localStorage and cache) or by uninstalling the PWA, if you installed it.

External links

If you open links to external sites (for example, DOI/Zenodo or ORCID), their own privacy policies apply.
